task_completion.description: Verify that the four required NACA airfoils (0015, 2415, 4415, 6415) have been generated in QBlade.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the QBlade interface showing the four airfoils (NACA 0015, NACA 2415, NACA 4415, NACA 6415) listed in the project tree, airfoil manager, or displayed in the main viewing area.

task_completion.description: Verify that the XFOIL Direct Analysis parameters are set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a polar definition or analysis setup dialog in QBlade showing Reynolds Number = 500,000, Ncrit = 9, and an Alpha range from -5 to 20 degrees with a step of 0.5.

task_completion.description: Verify that aerodynamic polars have been generated for all four airfoils.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for polar graphs (e.g., Cl/Cd vs Alpha) in QBlade displaying curves for all four airfoils, or check the project tree for generated polar data objects corresponding to each airfoil.

task_completion.description: Verify that the camber study report is created at the correct path with the required format and identifies the best airfoil.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor showing a file named 'camber_study_report.txt' (path /home/ga/Documents/camber_study_report.txt). The content must include lines formatted exactly as 'NACA [XXXX]: L/D_max = [value], at alpha = [value] deg' for each airfoil, and a final line 'BEST AIRFOIL: NACA [XXXX]'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the QBlade project is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the QBlade window title bar showing 'camber_study.wpa', a save dialog confirming the path '/home/ga/Documents/projects/camber_study.wpa', or a file manager showing the saved .wpa file in the projects directory.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually used the QBlade GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the agent clicking through QBlade menus, interacting with dialog boxes, and viewing graphs, rather than just running terminal commands or writing a python script to bypass the software.

integrity.description: Verify that the L/D_max values in the report correspond to the actual analysis results in QBlade.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent viewing the polar graphs or data tables in QBlade to read the maximum Cl/Cd (L/D) values before typing them into the report. The values in the text file should match the peaks shown in the QBlade plots.

integrity.description: Verify that the report and project files were genuinely saved to the disk.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard save dialogs, text editor save actions, or terminal commands (like 'ls') confirming the files exist at the exact requested paths, rather than just being typed out in an unsaved editor buffer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using QBlade's integrated XFOIL solver. Required airfoils: NACA 0015, 2415, 4415, 6415. Analysis parameters: Reynolds number = 500,000, Ncrit = 9, Alpha range = -5 to 20 degrees. Report path: /home/ga/Documents/camber_study_report.txt. Project path: /home/ga/Documents/projects/camber_study.wpa. The report must contain the exact string format 'NACA [XXXX]: L/D_max = [value], at alpha = [value] deg' and 'BEST AIRFOIL: NACA [XXXX]'.
